titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Vincent Peranio Papers
titlestmt.author: by Emma Gronbeck

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This collection contains 12 published digital objects. And those objects are comprised of 454 digital files.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday January 20, 2023 at 04:02

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Vincent Peranio Papers consists of correspondence, legal and financial records, scripts, photographs, props, miniature set models, concept art, architectural plans, ephemera, computer media, one audiocassette, and other materials documenting the career of production designer and art director Vincent Peranio. Peranio had a close working relationship with American director John Waters, and included are materials related to the production of Waters movies such as Polyester (1981), Hairspray (1988), Cry-Baby (1990), and Serial Mom (1994). Also present are materials related to Peranio's work on a number of Baltimore-based crime drama and police procedural television series, including David Simon's The Corner (2000) and The Wire (2002-2008), Homicide: Life on the Street (1993-1999), and Unforgettable (2011-2016). The collection additionally contains production materials for over a dozen other films and television series by directors including Steve Antin, Joe Berlinger, John David Coles, Agnieszka Holland, Barry Levinson, Michael Preece, Mark Rosman, Shawn Ryan, Richard C. Sarafian, and Joseph Sargent.

bioghist.head: Vincent Peranio (1945-)
bioghist.p: Vincent Peranio (1945-) is an American film and television production designer and art director. Born in 1945 in Baltimore, Maryland, he earned a Master of Fine Arts from the Maryland Institute College of Art in 1968 and met filmmaker John Waters shortly thereafter. Peranio became part of a group later known as the "Dreamlanders," a term coined for regular members of the cast and crew of Waters's early films. After designing the costume for—and playing the part of—Lobstora in Waters's second feature-length film, Multiple Maniacs (1970), Peranio served as production designer for all of Waters's subsequent Baltimore-based feature films, such as Hairspray (1988) and Cry-Baby (1990). Other films on which Peranio has worked are Book of Shadows: Blair Witch 2 (2000), the Blaxploitation film The Hitter (1979), and cult horror film The House on Sorority Row (1982). His notable TV series include Homicide: Life on the Street (1993-1999) and The Wire (2002-2008).

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The Vincent Peranio papers consists of correspondence, legal and financial records, scripts, photographs, props, miniature set models, concept art, architectural plans, ephemera, computer media, one audiocassette, and other materials documenting the career of production designer and art director Vincent Peranio. Peranio had a close working relationship with American director John Waters, and included are materials related to the production of Waters movies such as Polyester (1981), Hairspray (1988), Cry-Baby (1990), and Serial Mom (1994). Also present are materials related to Peranio's work on a number of Baltimore-based crime drama and police procedural television series, including David Simon's The Corner (2000) and The Wire (2002-2008), Homicide: Life on the Street (1993-1999), and Unforgettable (2011-2016). The collection additionally contains production materials for over a dozen other films and television series by directors including Steve Antin, Joe Berlinger, John David Coles, Agnieszka Holland, Barry Levinson, Michael Preece, Mark Rosman, Shawn Ryan, Richard C. Sarafian, and Joseph Sargent.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: I. John Waters Projects, 1966-2014. II. David Simon Projects, 1999-2017. III. Homicide: Life on the Street (1993-1999), 1992-1999. IV. Other Projects, 1970-2014.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit. Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing. This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 59 (audiovisual material and computer media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Vincent Peranio Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Royal Books on the Sinclair Lewis Fund, 2019.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Vincent Peranio Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains production and promotional materials for eleven feature films and two short films directed by John Waters. Materials include correspondence, script drafts and revisions, shooting schedules, production photographs, props, concept art, architectural plans, production notes, cast, crew, and contact lists, promotional flyers and posters, and premiere announcements and invitations. Some of the most well-documented films in this series are Hairspray (1988), Cry-Baby (1990), Serial Mom (1994), and Cecil B. DeMented (2000).

did.unittitle: Set lists, floor plans, and prop notes
Photographs, cast, Kathleen Turner and Sam Waterson
Photographs, interior home set or location
Props, fake book jacket for "Serial Mom" novel
Props, yellow vinyl banner advertising the "Camel Lips" show at Hammerjacks
Concept art, sketches and architectural plans
One line schedules and scout itineraries

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains production materials for two HBO crime dramas written by David Simon, The Corner (2000) and The Wire (2002-2008), including script drafts and revisions, shooting schedules, set and location photographs, concept art, production notes, crew and contact lists, and architectural plans.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: The Corner (2000), and The Wire (2002-2008).

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 59 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of production materials for the six-episode HBO miniseries The Corner (2000), including script drafts and revisions, shooting schedules, one line schedules, set and location photographs, contact lists, location notes, and ephemera.

did.unittitle: Final draft, typescript and location list
Script, Part 4, "Dope Fiend Blues," draft, typescript
Script, Part 5, "Corner Boy Blues," first draft, typescript
Script, Part 6, "Everyman's Blues," first draft, typescript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of production materials for all five seasons of the HBO crime drama The Wire (2002-2008). Seasons one and four are the most fully represented, with a few materials related to seasons two, three, and five. Materials include script drafts and revisions, shooting schedules, one line schedules, crew and location contact lists, concept art, architectural plans, set and location photographs, printed materials, production notes, production ephemera, several miniature set models, and an audiocassette containing music used in the series.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 59 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of production materials for all seven seasons of the NBC police procedural, Homicide: Life on the Street (1993-1999), as well as the television movie, Homicide: The Movie (2000), which concluded the TV series. Materials include script drafts and revisions, one line schedules, shooting schedules, cast and crew lists, set lists, concept art, set and location photographs, production ephemera, and some small props such as business cards, photographs, and bullet shells. Some episode scripts for seasons five, six, and seven are not present.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of other projects involving Peranio, such as film productions, television productions, architectural design work, and art exhibitions. Included are correspondence, lists and schedules, scripts, photographs and artwork, financial records, and printed materials.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Film, Television, and Non-Film Work.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 59 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of production and promotional materials for sixteen motion picture and television films featuring Peranio's work, including correspondence; script drafts and revisions; shooting and one line schedules; production lists and notes; cast, crew, and contact lists; and budgets and expenses. Visual materials include production photographs, concept art, architectural plans, and reference images and photographs. Also included are promotional flyers, posters, premiere announcements, and invitations. Among the most well-documented films in this subseries are John David Coles's Darrow (1991), Agnieszka Holland's Shot in the Heart (2001), and Barry Levinson's Liberty Heights (1999). Other notable materials include the "Black Bible" of story information and timelines for Joe Berlinger's The Book of Shadows: Blair Witch 2 (2000), concept art and script drafts for Mark Rosman's The House on Sorority Row (1982), and a scale model of a surgical theater from Joseph Sargent's Something the Lord Made (2004).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by director and sub-arranged chronologically by production.

did.unittitle: Berlinger, Joe, Book of Shadows: Blair Witch 2 (2000)
"The Blair Witch 'Black Bible'," compiled by Ben Rock

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A document detailing the legend of the "Blair Witch," as well as listing all important characters and events from the films and associated media.

did.unittitle: Ephemera, premiere invitation and Peranio crew ID
Bonner, Lee, The Adventures of the Action Hunters (1987)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials are identified by the working title "Two for the Money."

did.unittitle: Currier, Lavinia, Heart of the Garden (1985)
Script, final draft, third revision, typescript
Hanson, Curtis, The Bedroom Window (1987), script, second draft, typescript
Holland, Agnieszka, Shot in the Heart (2001)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials are identified by the working title "Don't Call Me Boy."

did.unittitle: "The Gaiety Burlesque Show," set and shooting

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials are identified by the working title "Boy of Pigs."

did.unittitle: Script, final draft, typescript, with revisions
Rosman, Mark, The House on Sorority Row (1982)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Some materials are identified by the working title "Seven Sisters."

did.unittitle: Sargent, Joseph, Something the Lord Made (2004)
Photographs, sets and locations, with negatives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of materials related to Peranio's production designs for five television programs, including script drafts and revisions, shooting schedules, production photographs, concept art, architectural plans, production lists and notes, reference images and photographs, and crew and contact lists. The most well-documented television program in this subseries is John Bellucci's Unforgettable (2011-2016), for which there are a large number of architectural plans and concept art sketches and illustrations.

did.unittitle: Episode #121, architectural plans and concept art
Fogel, Susanna and Jone Lefkowitz, The Washingtonienne
Schweitzer, Michael, "Out of Time" (1985) ( NBC Special Treat , Season 9, Episode 3)

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 59 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of materials related to Vincent Peranio's other projects, including redesigns of the interiors of the Club Charles and Playhouse Theater in Baltimore, and exhibitions on the films of John Waters and Peranio's work in production design. Materials include concept art, notes, exhibition ephemera and press material, photographs, and correspondence.

did.unittitle: Baltimore, Club Charles redesign, concept art
Baltimore, Club Charles redesign, notes and concept art
Exhibition, "John Waters: Change of Life," New Museum of Contemporary Art, NYC
Exhibition, "Trash to Treasure: The Production Design of Vince Peranio," Creative Alliance at The Patterson, Baltimore
John Waters, "This Filthy World," Comedy Central, concept art and set photos
John Waters, "This Filthy World," Comedy Central, concept art illustration
